findings.message: All factual script spans link to grade A/B claims; every cited claim exists in the ledger; all 8 ledger claims used; no E-grade claims in the script.
Both critical claims (CLM-0001 holding, CLM-0002 date/vote/author) are supported by the primary opinion (directness 5).
Cold-open hook promises are all paid off in the body: thrown-out confession -> Act III (CLM-0001); won yet jailed -> Act IV/Ending (CLM-0006); four sentences -> Act III/IV (CLM-0004/0008).
All 23 script spans are mapped to scenes; no orphan scenes; planned duration ~745s (within the 12-min format).

Research QC is 'warn': the CourtListener opinion's durable content hash was not captured (API needs COURTLISTENER_TOKEN; HTTP 401). The published citation 384 U.S. 436 is valid, but capture the hash and recheck volatile facts before publish.

Six scenes depict the real person / case as symbolic reconstruction (Ernesto Miranda, CLM-0006). Human review required before render/publish; phrasing must keep 'conviction reversed, then reconvicted on other evidence' and must NOT imply exoneration (docs/32, invariant 11).

Episode-specific Midjourney stills (5) and reusable motifs are not generated yet, and the Suno music library is empty; the full render uses fallback placeholders until owner generates and Claude selects/registers them.
Narration audio is not generated (ElevenLabs is paid; voice_plan v001 is a $0 plan only). Master generation requires owner cost approval and the script-approved revision (APR-0001).
Reconstructions/illustrations must carry the synthetic-media + 'not legal advice' disclaimer (cold-open line + description) before publish (decisions/0002 §5, docs/13/32).
